verdict: AI displacement risk is moderate, with some tasks being automated but human judgment still essential.

reasoning_steps.title: High routine_cognitive score
reasoning_steps.body: Task 1 (Sentence defendants in criminal cases) and Task 6 (Rule on admissibility of evidence) are highly automated, with Claude capable of drafting motions and extracting citations.

reasoning_steps.title: Strong regulatory_moat
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks 2 (Monitor proceedings) and 11 (Issue arrest warrants) require human judgment to ensure compliance with laws and procedures.

reasoning_steps.title: Moderate creative_judgment score
reasoning_steps.body: While tasks like Task 3 (Instruct juries) and Task 10 (Research legal issues) require some creativity, they are not as demanding as those in other occupations.

adjacent_careers.name: Lawyer (Litigator)
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Both judges and lawyers rely on their ability to interpret the law and argue cases, but lawyers typically handle more adversarial work.

adjacent_careers.name: Prosecutor
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Prosecutors and judges share similar responsibilities in the justice system, but prosecutors focus on presenting cases to a jury.

adjacent_careers.name: Defender (Attorney)
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Defense attorneys and judges work together to ensure that defendants receive fair treatment, but defense attorneys handle more adversarial work.

recent_developments.headline: New AI-powered court management system launched
recent_developments.impact: This system will automate some tasks, such as document review and evidence management, but human judges will still be involved in decision-making.

recent_developments.headline: AI contract review reaches enterprise adoption
recent_developments.impact: Top-100 law firms report cutting document review hours by 60–80% on standard agreements.

quotes.quote: Lawyers who use AI will replace lawyers who do not. The profession is not going away, but the structure of how legal work gets done is going to change in a very significant way.
quotes.speaker: Richard Susskind
quotes.title: Author, Tomorrow's Lawyers; technology adviser to the Lord Chief Justice of England

quotes.quote: Document review, due diligence, and first-draft contract work are the parts of legal practice most exposed. Counseling and courtroom judgment are the most insulated.
quotes.speaker: Daniel Susskind
quotes.title: Author, A World Without Work; Oxford economist

quotes.quote: We expect roughly 44 percent of legal work tasks to be automatable by current LLMs. That does not mean 44 percent fewer lawyers — it means restructured firms.
quotes.speaker: Goldman Sachs Research
quotes.title: Economic research note
quotes.source: Goldman Sachs: AI Exposure of Occupations
